title: phyteuma scheuchzeri subsp. charmelioides
intro: Phyteuma scheuchzeri subsp. charmelioides, also known as Alpine Cabbage or Charmelion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phyteuma scheuchzeri subsp. charmelioides include its ability to treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using them in tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
